steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The blank falls between two independent clauses: an interrogative clause on the left (“What are atmospheric rivers, and how...”) and a declarative clause on the right (“Part of the water cycle, atmospheric rivers are...”).

steps.title: Apply question syntax and boundary rules
steps.content: The interrogative clause requires the auxiliary verb to come before the subject (“do they affect” rather than “they do affect”) and must end with a question mark to properly separate it from the following statement. Choice C provides both correct word order and the required question mark. The other options either use a period instead of a question mark (A and B) or reverse the word order (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
